description_long: This is a large, two-handed sword with a distinctive wavy blade, reminiscent of a flamberge or flame-bladed sword. The blade is long, dark, and undulates along its length, giving it a striking and aggressive appearance. The crossguard is ornate, with quillons that curve upward and outward, providing both protection and a decorative flourish. The handle is cylindrical, featuring a lighter central grip section flanked by darker pommel and guard ends, and ends in a rounded pommel with a small ring or loop. The materials appear to be primarily metal for the blade and guard, with the grip possibly wrapped or constructed from a lighter material for comfort. This sword is visually imposing and would stand out as a ceremonial or high-status weapon in an urban context.
description: A large, wavy-bladed two-handed sword with an ornate crossguard and a cylindrical handle featuring a ringed pommel. The blade is dark and undulating, giving it a dramatic and ceremonial look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wavy blade, ornate crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a ringed pommel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the symmetrical crossguard, with the handle and pommel visible end-on.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, showing the undulating blade and the details of the crossguard and handle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handle and pommel, with the blade extending away and the crossguard arms visible from behind.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, partially wrapped or banded, with a rounded pommel and a small ring
attribute_ornamentation: decorative crossguard and pommel, contrasting grip bands
